meta.description: Metadata required for a cursor-paginated response.

next.description: The cursor to be provided in the next request to fetch the next page

View the successfully uploaded documents uploaded for this user.

post.description:
This is a preview API. By using this, you consent to potentially breaking API changes.
Checks if provided data is valid before adding it to the vault.

This is a preview API. By using this, you consent to potentially breaking API changes.
Decrypt complex objects in place. Like the vault proxy endpoints, but reflects back the hydrated request to the caller.

parameters.name: x-fp-id
parameters.description: When reflect requests are on behalf of a single footprint vault, you can can omit the `fp_id_` prefix on token identifiers, and just use `id.x` or `custom.y` instead of `fp_id_xyz.id.x` or `fp_id_xyz.custom.y`.

parameters.name: x-fp-access-reason
parameters.description: Access reason for any decryption operations during the reflection request

Dashboard Token.name: X-Fp-Dashboard-Authorization
Dashboard Token.description: Short-lived token for an authenticated dashboard user.

Secret API Key.name: X-Footprint-Secret-Key
Secret API Key.description: Secret API key. You can create and view your API keys in the dashboard. This key should never be sent to your client and should be treated as a secret on your server.

account_id.description: The associated user's alpaca account id

api_key.description: API Key to use with alpaca

api_secret.description: API Secret to use with alpaca

default_approver.description:
The default approver name/email to use for automatically approved users
 This will be overwritten if done by a manual reviewer

fp_user_id.description: the footprint user id on behalf of which to send the request

hostname.description:
Alpaca Hostname to use (i.e. sandbox or production like: )
 for example: `broker-api.sandbox.alpaca.markets`

items.description: In order to comply with Alpaca’s terms of service, each account owner must be presented the following agreements.

api_key.description: API Key to use with alpaca

api_secret.description: API Secret to use with alpaca

disclosures.description: It is your responsibility as the service provider to denote if the account owner falls under each category defined by FINRA rules. We recommend asking these questions at any point of the onboarding process of each account owner in the form of Y/N and Radio Buttons.

context.description: Information relevant to the user’s disclosure selection should be sent through this object.

items.description: If you utilize Alpaca for KYCaaS, additional information will need to be submitted if the user identifies with any of the disclosures before the account can be approved. This information can be sent through the context object to speed up the time to approve their account.

company_city.description: Required if context_type = AFFILIATE_FIRM or CONTROLLED_FIRM

company_compliance_email.description: Required if context_type = AFFILIATE_FIRM or CONTROLLED_FIRM

company_country.description: Required if context_type = AFFILIATE_FIRM or CONTROLLED_FIRM

company_name.description: Required if context_type = AFFILIATE_FIRM or CONTROLLED_FIRM

company_street_address.description: Required if context_type = AFFILIATE_FIRM or CONTROLLED_FIRM

immediate_family_exposed.description: If your user’s immediate family member (sibling, husband/wife, child, parent) is either politically exposed or holds a control position.

is_control_person.description: Whether user holds a controlling position in a publicly traded company, member of the board of directors or has policy making abilities in a publicly traded company.

fp_user_id.description: the footprint user id on behalf of which to send the request

hostname.description:
Alpaca Hostname to use (i.e. sandbox or production like: )
 for example: `broker-api.sandbox.alpaca.markets`

trusted_contact.description: This model input is optional. However, the client should make reasonable effort to obtain the trusted contact information. See more details in FINRA Notice 17-11

email_address.description: In addition, only one of the following is required:

default_approver.description:
The default approver name/email to use for automatically approved users
 This will be overwritten if done by a manual reviewer

fp_user_id.description: the footprint user id on behalf of which to send the request

fields.description: List of data identifiers to decrypt. For example, `id.first_name`, `id.ssn4`, `custom.bank_account`

items.description:
Represents a piece of data stored inside the user vault.
 Mostly used in requests to decrypt a piece of data and in access events to show the log of decrypted items.

signing_key.description: A hex-encoded key for computing `hmac-sha256` signatures

document_type.description: Document type of the successfully uploaded document. Can be used to fetch from vault

note.description: Short description of the reason code

scopes.description: What the reason code applies to (Name, Document, Business, etc)

items.description:
Represents the granularity of data attributes that could be alerted on by a data vendor
 NOTE: this is not the same as "data attributes we can collect from a user". Please see `DataAttribute` for that

RiskSignal.description: RiskSignal information, including severity, impacted scopes, and more.

items.description:
Represents the granularity of data attributes that could be alerted on by a data vendor
 NOTE: this is not the same as "data attributes we can collect from a user". Please see `DataAttribute` for that
